latest.title: Presentation at the UK Mining Conference
latest.content:
9 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5496H
Tungsten West PLC
09 June 2026
09 June 2026
Tungsten West Plc
("Tungsten West", the "Company" or the "Group")
Presentation at the UK Mining Conference
Tungsten West (AIM:TUN), the mining company focused on restarting production at the Hemerdon tungsten and tin mine ("Hemerdon" or the "Project") in Devon, UK, is pleased to announce that Phil Povey, Chief Financial Officer, will be attending the UK Mining Conference in Cornwall this week.
The Company will present at the pre-conference Investors' Congress today and at the main conference on Wednesday 10 June 2026. The presentation will focus on the progress being made at Hemerdon as it gets closer to restarting production, unlocking critical minerals and creating a secure supply of tungsten and tin in the UK.
Presentation times
Pre-Conference Event: Investors' Congress: Tuesday 9 June 2026 at 11:00am
Conference Event: Speaker Session: Wednesday 10 June 2026 at 12:35pm
The Investors' Congress is free to private and professional investors consisting of a half-day event that explores how, why, and when to invest in the mining sector, helping investors identify the hidden value in mining companies.
Ends
For further information, please contact:
Tungsten West
Jeff Court, Chief Executive Officer
Phil Povey, Chief Financial Officer

latest.title: Q1 2026 Project Funding and Debt Update
latest.content:
21 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2493F
Tungsten West PLC
21 May 2026
21 May 2026
Tungsten West Plc
("Tungsten West", the "Company" or the "Group")
Q1 2026 Project Funding and Debt Update
Tungsten West (AIM:TUN), the mining company focused on restarting production at the Hemerdon tungsten and tin mine ("Hemerdon" or the "Project") in Devon, UK, is pleased to provide a Project Funding and debt update.
Project Funding Update
The Company has entered into a binding US$25.0 million bridging loan facility (the "Short-Term Loan Facility") with an entity controlled by Gregory Coffey. As previously announced, the Short-Term Loan Facility will fund the Project up to the first phase restart of fines gravity processing in Q3 2026.
The key terms of the Short-Term Loan Facility are:
·
US$25.0 million;
·
Interest Rate of SOFR + 4.5% (approximately 8% per annum), increasing by 1% per quarter;
·
Term of 366 days; and
·
Unsecured and repayable at any time, without penalty or make whole fee.
Final stage due diligence of the larger debt package of up to US$85.0 million (the "Facility") is complete, and definitive documentation for the Facility is being finalised with the lender, which will be partially utilised to repay any funds provided under the Short-Term Loan Facility.
Project and Market Update
·
The Company remains on track to deliver the restart of the Project within budget on the previously announced timelines:
o
Fines gravity circuit refurbishment is well progressed, and commissioning is expected in Q3 2026. Fines will be generated using a third-party mobile crushing and wash plant solution, with feedstock sourced from the previous operators' ore stockpiles. The fines gravity circuit will initially be run at up to 100 tonnes ("t") per hour (fines gravity installed capacity is approximately 200t per hour). The Company has entered into an Agreement with Duo Operations Limited to provide mobile crushing and wash plant services on a
variable cost basis.
o
Coarse gravity circuit refurbishment is progressing well, with commissioning expected in Q4 2026. The initial flow sheet will consist of pre-concentration using a mobile in line pressure jig system and the previously installed Dense Media Separation ("DMS") system. Feedstock will be sourced from exposed ore, with minimal stripping. Although initial throughput will be lower, in this configuration, the coarse gravity circuit will be capable of processing approximately 150t per hour.
o
Full project commissioning is expected in Q1 2027. This will allow the Company to ramp up to nameplate capacity of 500t per hour over the course of 2027.
·
The tungsten concentrates market remains very strong, with pricing by the European market index pricing ("APT") consistently above US$3,000/metric tonne unit. In relation to offtakes, the Company has well progressed discussions with multiple parties.
·
A recruitment programme is ongoing, with over 120 new personnel expected to have been onboarded by the end of June 2026.
·
Six pieces of Komatsu heavy mobile equipment are currently onsite and conducting preparatory mining works. The first fleet of Komatsu primary earth moving equipment is expected to arrive at site in June 2026.
·
In response to recent market conditions, the Company has commenced a project to materially increase the production capacity of the operation, including significant additional capacity in the new build programme currently underway.
Related party transaction
As the entity is controlled by Gregory Coffey, a substantial shareholder of the Company, the transaction will constitute a related party transaction under Rule 13 of the AIM Rules for Companies.
The Company's Directors consider, having consulted with Strand Hanson Limited, acting in its capacity as the Company's Nominated Adviser, that the terms of the Short-Term Loan Facility are fair and reasonable insofar as the Company's shareholders are concerned.
Jeff Court, CEO of
Tungsten West, commented:
"We are thankful for the support of our shareholder, who has provided the bridging loan facility on favourable terms. Over the coming weeks, we look forward to concluding our remaining debt financing package, which will allow us to maintain our rapid development pace and deliver tungsten and tin concentrate into the market from Q3 2026."
Mineral Processing Facility project works underway at Hemerdon with the mining operation in the background.
Ends
For further information, please contact:
Tungsten West
Jeff Court, Chief Executive Officer
Phil Povey, Chief Financial Officer
Tel: +44 (0) 1752 278500

(Nominated Adviser and Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: TUN.L is a mining company that focuses on extracting tungsten from underground deposits. The company also processes tungsten ore into refined products used in high‑tech industries such as electronics, aerospace, and steel manufacturing.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tungsten is a critical component in modern technology – it’s used in hard‑disk drives, LED lighting, and jet‑engine parts. Demand for tungsten is growing as electronics and green‑energy sectors expand. The company sits in a niche market where supply can be scarce, giving it a strategic advantage if it can bring a mine online.
company_30s.current_state: TUN.L is still in the development phase. It has raised capital through several share issuances and convertible notes, but it has not yet started commercial production. The business is burning cash to secure mining rights, build infrastructure, and pay down debt. The share price is trading at a fraction of the valuation implied by its resource estimates.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy raising money – a bridge loan, a book‑build, and a retail offer all point to an aggressive fundraising push. These funds are earmarked for project development and debt refinancing, signalling an intent to move from exploration to production.
Corporate governance is in flux: new board members, major holdings announced, and director dealings suggest that the leadership is trying to align incentives and attract institutional interest.

The CFO’s presentation at the UK Mining Conference and the Q1 funding update are the main public touchpoints. They show that management is actively courting investors and trying to prove that the project can deliver returns.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – a small group of traders sees a turnaround coming but the wider market is still silent.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter comes from a handful of traders who praise the CFO’s track record and view the company as a long‑term buy. They highlight the company’s potential to tap into a high‑demand commodity and the recent funding as signs of confidence.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The few bearish voices point to the lack of production, heavy debt load, and the fact that the company hasn’t yet proven it can extract and sell tungsten profitably.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Bullish chatter has risen slightly after the conference presentation, but overall volume remains low. The market is still waiting for a concrete catalyst before committing capital.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no public interest is currently being tracked. This means the market could be surprised if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the scheduled funding round – a successful book‑build would inject the cash needed to start construction.
Announcement of a first‑stage production target or a milestone report from the mine site.
A rise in tungsten spot prices above $X per kilogram, which would improve the project’s economics.
A volume spike at the current support level (around 1p) that shows institutional buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained increase in daily volume, a break of the 1p support, and any press release that confirms a production start date or a new financing tranche.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score suggests the price is in a tight compression zone after a deep drop, typical of early‑capitulation stages. The next move will likely be a bounce off the 1p level once a catalyst – such as funding or a production milestone – materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has plunged well beyond 70% from its peak, placing it in the high‑reward crash zone. If the company follows through on its funding and production plans, the price could recover to roughly the midpoint of its recent range – a 300–500% upside from the current level. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the market has already punished the stock for a long time and there’s little reason for further pain once the catalysts arrive.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the company never brings a mine online or that the funding falls through. A failure to secure additional capital would push the price further into the 0.5p territory. The worst case is a continued decline to zero, which would wipe out any remaining equity.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose the current 1p price, but a recovery to 3p would double that. A target of 5p would give you a 400% return. The upside far outweighs the small downside, making the trade asymmetrical in your favour.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the 1p support once volume is at least 2× the 20‑day average. Use a 20% stop below entry to protect against a false breakout. Allocate 10% of your portfolio to this position and add a trailing stop that locks in gains once the price moves above 2p.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume spike at 1p, a funding announcement, or a production milestone release. These signals will confirm that the catalysts are in place.
playbook.if_passing: There are no immediate terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy. The only thing that could cause the trade to fail is a complete collapse of the project’s economics, which would likely be visible through a funding failure or a sharp drop in tungsten prices.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, awaiting funding or production milestones, high upside with limited downside – a clear contrarian bullish case.
